series_metadata.series_description: The Deceased Injured Worker (here after known as DIW) Claim Files contains documents related to a claim for workers’ compensation benefits due to a work-related injury resulting in the death of an employee. These Claim files may include (but are not limited to); Dependent death benefits, all correspondence, BWC Orders, IC Orders, Family Support Orders, Forms: FROI-1 the First Report of Injury, C-101 Authorization to Release Medical Information, C-86 Motion of Claimant, IC Hearing Documents, C-92 Percentage Applications and Awards, medical documents, Medical Peer Reviews, Medical and non-medical Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) packets, x-ray interpretations, psychological reports, C-84 Request for Temporary Total Compensation, Permanent Total Disability Compensation Application, Vocational Rehabilitation Reports, Wage Loss Application (working and non-working wage loss), Living Maintenance Compensation Application, Complete Settlement Packets and Settlement Agreement, Disability Relief and Disability Relief Award Orders. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after with DOI on or after 9/15/2020 on a Medical Only Claim, the claim expires five (5) years from the date of the last medical service rendered, compensation payment, or date of death. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after 08/25/2006 to 9/14/2020 on a Medical Only Claim: Retain for six (6) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, date of death, or six (6) years from the date of injury if no medical payments. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after 12/11/1967 to 8/24/2006 on a Medical Only Claim: Retain for six (6) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, date of death, or six (6) years from the date of injury if no medical payments. • Retain DIW Claims for Date of Injury Prior to 12/11/1967 on a Medical Only Claim: Retain ten (10) years from last medical bill, compensation payment, date of death, or ten (10) years from the date of injury if no medical payments. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after with DOI on or after 9/15/2020 on a Lost Time Claim, the claim expires five (5) years from the date of the last medical service rendered, compensation payment, or date of death. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after 08/25/2006 to 9/14/2020 on a Lost Time Claim: Retain for six (6) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, or date of death, or six (6) years from the date of injury if no medical payments. • Retain DIW Claims with date of injury on or after 12/11/1967 to 8/24/2006 on a Lost Time Claim: Retain for six (6) years from the last medical bill, compensation payment, date of death, or six (6) years from the date of injury if no medical payments. • Retain DIW Claims for Date of Injury Prior to 12/11/1967 on a Lost Time Claim: Retain ten (10) years from last medical bill, compensation payment, date of death, or ten (10) years from the date of injury if no medical payments.

retention_rules.trigger_event: Microform: Digitally image claim mail into claim file, verify a clear copy and ; Electronic: Digitally image into appropriate claims file, hold according appropriate statutory time frame (listed in description above); and ; Paper: Digitally image into appropriate claims file, hold according appropriate statutory time frame (listed in description above); and
retention_rules.disposition_method: Microform: Burn ; Electronic: Delete ; Paper: Shred
